name: Sinch
what_it_does: Multi-channel communication platform (owner of Mailgun and Mailjet) offering SMS, voice, WhatsApp, and email APIs through a unified cloud communications platform for programmatic customer engagement.
use_cases: Sending transactional SMS notifications for OTP, alerts, and status updates
Building voice call automation for IVR systems and outbound call workflows
Sending WhatsApp Business messages via the Sinch unified API
Number lookup and phone number verification for user onboarding
Combining SMS + email + voice in a single multi-channel communication workflow
not_for: Email-only workflows (Mailgun directly is simpler if you only need email)
In-app messaging or chat (use Sendbird or Stream for in-app messaging)
Organizations needing primarily carrier-grade voice infrastructure (use Twilio or Bandwidth)
Very small-scale usage where per-message costs dominate
best_when: You need multi-channel communication (SMS + voice + email + WhatsApp) from a single vendor with competitive pricing and global carrier coverage.
avoid_when: You only need one channel (use the best-in-class single-channel provider), or you need advanced programmable voice features that Twilio does better.

auth.notes: Service-specific keys (SMS uses different key format than voice). Application key and secret pair for most services. API keys are service-scoped — SMS key doesn't work for voice API.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free trial credits for testing; varies by product
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-message/minute pricing; varies significantly by country and channel
pricing.notes: Pricing varies widely by destination country, channel, and volume. Trial credits available. Enterprise volume discounts negotiated directly.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by product; SMS typically throttled by carrier limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns standard HTTP codes with JSON error details. Error messages are functional but vary in detail across different product APIs.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: SMS sending is not idempotent — sending twice sends two messages. Status queries are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Sinch has acquired many companies (Mailgun, Mailjet, MessageBird) — different products have different API styles and auth
Service-specific API keys don't work across products — agents need separate credentials per service
Phone number provisioning is separate from messaging — provision numbers before sending
Country-specific regulations affect deliverability — agent workflows must handle country-specific restrictions
Webhook delivery for delivery receipts uses different URL paths per service — configure per-product webhook URLs
